content.summary:
User asked in Russian to verify availability of `gpt-5.4-mini` and then update OpenCode configs accordingly: `opencode/opencode.jsonc`, `opencode/oh-my-openagent_openai.jsonc`, and `opencode/oh-my-openagent_openai-google-hybrid.jsonc`. User clarified that the check must be against actual OpenCode runtime under current OpenAI Codex/OAuth authorization, not just static config presence. They want to keep available models restricted to avoid inaccessible junk models, and explicitly approved adding `gpt-5.4-mini` to the OpenAI whitelist and adding a model definition to `opencode.jsonc`. They also provided extensive Oh My OpenAgent guidance stating `gpt-5.4-mini` is appropriate for quick/utility roles, while heavier planner/reviewer/orchestrator roles should stay on stronger models such as `gpt-5.4`.

Initial state read from files:
- `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode/opencode.jsonc` had `model: "openai/gpt-5.2"`; `provider.openai.whitelist` included `gpt-5.4`, `gpt-5.3-codex`, `gpt-5.2`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.1-codex-max`, `gpt-5.1-codex-mini`, but NOT `gpt-5.4-mini`; `provider.openai.models` had definitions for `gpt-5.2`, `gpt-5.2-codex`, `gpt-5.1-codex-max`, `gpt-5.1-codex-mini`; no `gpt-5.4-mini` entry.
- `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode/oh-my-openagent_openai.jsonc` already used `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` in many places: agents `librarian`, `explore`, `multimodal-looker`, `prometheus`, `momus`; categories `visual-engineering`, `quick`, `unspecified-low`, `writing`; background concurrency `"openai/gpt-5.4-mini": 8`.
- `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode/oh-my-openagent_openai-google-hybrid.jsonc` already used `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` only for `categories.quick` and background concurrency `"openai/gpt-5.4-mini": 6`; heavy roles already stayed on `openai/gpt-5.4` or Google models.

Intent/routing statements made during work:
- Classified as implementation task with required runtime verification before edits.
- Declared use of explore/librarian/plan agents before implementation.

Todos created and maintained:
1. Check gpt-5.4-mini availability via OpenAI OAuth in OpenCode.
2. Add gpt-5.4-mini to openai whitelist + models definition in `opencode.jsonc`.
3. Review/update `oh-my-openagent_openai.jsonc` if needed.
4. Review/update `oh-my-openagent_openai-google-hybrid.jsonc` if needed.
5. Verify all configs are valid JSONC after edits.
All todos were eventually marked completed.

Direct checks before editing:
- `opencode models 2>&1 | grep -i "gpt-5.4-mini" || echo "NOT FOUND IN MODELS LIST"` returned `NOT FOUND IN MODELS LIST`.
- `opencode models 2>&1 | grep -i "openai" | head -20` showed only: `openai/gpt-5.1-codex-max`, `openai/gpt-5.1-codex-mini`, `openai/gpt-5.2`, `openai/gpt-5.2-codex`, `openai/gpt-5.3-codex`, `openai/gpt-5.4`.
- `opencode --help`, `opencode auth --help`, `opencode run --help`, `opencode debug --help` were checked to understand CLI/runtime QA options.
- `opencode auth list` confirmed current auth included `OpenAI oauth`, `codex oauth`, `Google oauth`, plus other providers.

Background delegation and their outcomes:
- Explore background task `bg_8027e272` / session `ses_29ce58452ffethVr9i20B4p4hG` searched local repo and confirmed the critical issue: `gpt-5.4-mini` was already referenced in the Oh My OpenAgent configs but absent from `opencode.jsonc` whitelist. It also noted no need to touch Omniroute configs.
- Librarian background task `bg_e3fab31b` / session `ses_29ce58438ffeuioDzfDRuRZozU` was launched to research suitability of `gpt-5.4-mini`; returned only a partial initial session, so direct web lookups were used afterward.
- Plan task session `ses_29ce58425ffeNncMPXL35yl8ko` returned the actionable guidance: minimum safe edit if availability is confirmed is `opencode/opencode.jsonc`; verify with baseline `opencode models`, whitelist-gated retest, and tiny direct invocation; if unavailable/quota-limited, classify accordingly.

Additional local/external research performed:
- Grep over `opencode/` confirmed whitelist behavior and all local `gpt-5.4-mini` references.
- Search results and direct OpenAI docs confirmed `gpt-5.4-mini` is a real model intended for subagents/quick workloads.
- `https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models/gpt-5.4-mini` showed key specs used for config: `400,000 context window`, `128,000 max output tokens`, modalities `text,image` input and `text` output, and that it supports reasoning variants/tools. This doc also explicitly describes it as “Our strongest mini model yet for coding, computer use, and subagents.”
- `https://developers.openai.com/api/docs/models` showed OpenAI’s general recommendation: use `gpt-5.4` for complex reasoning/coding, choose `gpt-5.4-mini`/`nano` for lower-latency lower-cost workloads.
- GitHub code search also surfaced ecosystem references to `gpt-5.4-mini`, but the OpenAI docs were the key evidence.

Important reasoning decision before editing:
- Based on the user-supplied OmO guide, leaving `prometheus` and `momus` on `gpt-5.4-mini` in `oh-my-openagent_openai.jsonc` would contradict recommended role matching. Therefore the chosen minimal-but-correct change set became:
  1. open model access in `opencode.jsonc`
  2. keep `gpt-5.4-mini` on quick/utility roles
  3. move `prometheus` and `momus` in the OpenAI-only OmO config back to `openai/gpt-5.4`
  4. leave the hybrid config unchanged because it was already aligned (`quick` on mini, heavier roles on stronger models)

File edits actually applied:
1. `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode/opencode.jsonc`
   - Added `"gpt-5.4-mini",` to `provider.openai.whitelist` immediately after `"gpt-5.4",`.
   - Added a full `provider.openai.models["gpt-5.4-mini"]` block immediately after the `gpt-5.2` block with:
     - `"name": "GPT 5.4 Mini (OAuth)"`
     - `"limit": { "context": 400000, "output": 128000 }`
     - `"modalities": { "input": ["text", "image"], "output": ["text"] }`
     - `variants.none/low/medium/high/xhigh` mirroring the existing reasoning/textVerbosity pattern used by `gpt-5.2`.
2. `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode/oh-my-openagent_openai.jsonc`
   - Changed `agents.prometheus.model` from `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` to `openai/gpt-5.4`.
   - Changed `agents.momus.model` from `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` to `openai/gpt-5.4`.
3. `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode/oh-my-openagent_openai-google-hybrid.jsonc`
   - No edit made; it was explicitly reviewed and deemed already appropriate because `quick` already used `openai/gpt-5.4-mini` and heavy roles already used `openai/gpt-5.4` or Google models.

Post-edit verification performed:
- Re-read `opencode.jsonc` and `oh-my-openagent_openai.jsonc` to confirm exact inserted values.
- `opencode models openai` after the edit returned:
  - `openai/gpt-5.1-codex-max`
  - `openai/gpt-5.1-codex-mini`
  - `openai/gpt-5.2`
  - `openai/gpt-5.2-codex`
  - `openai/gpt-5.3-codex`
  - `openai/gpt-5.4`
  - `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`
  This proved the whitelist/config gate was resolved.
- `lsp_diagnostics` run on directory `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode` reported `Files with errors: 0`, though it scanned markdown only because of tool behavior on the directory.

Runtime/manual QA performed for actual model usability under current auth:
1. `opencode run -m openai/gpt-5.4-mini "Reply with exactly: OK"`
   - Output showed session banner `> Sisyphus (Ultraworker) · gpt-5.4-mini`
   - Command timed out after 120s.
   - This demonstrated that OpenCode accepted and started a session with `gpt-5.4-mini`; it did NOT fail on unknown model or immediate auth rejection.
2. `opencode --pure run -m openai/gpt-5.4-mini --format json "Reply with exactly: OK"`
   - Timed out after 180s with no useful visible payload.
3. `opencode --pure run -m openai/gpt-5.4-mini --print-logs "Reply with exactly: OK"`
   - Produced detailed logs showing core proof of runtime resolution:
     - provider discovery found `providerID=openai`
     - `service=llm providerID=openai modelID=gpt-5.4-mini sessionID=... stream`
     - terminal banner showed `> build · gpt-5.4-mini`
   - Output was huge and truncated to `/Users/tim/.local/share/opencode/tool-output/tool_d632944c2001sCOJqMkujblohq`.
   - No unknown-model error or immediate auth/config error appeared before truncation/timeout.

State at the point of compression:
- Work concluded that `gpt-5.4-mini` is now exposed in OpenCode and demonstrably resolves/starts under the user’s current OpenAI OAuth setup.
- However, the run command did not complete within timeout, so there is no final generated `OK` response captured yet. The evidence supports classification as “available and runtime-resolved,” not “proven to complete a full response within current timeout.”
- Large debug log file exists at `/Users/tim/.local/share/opencode/tool-output/tool_d632944c2001sCOJqMkujblohq` if later inspection is needed.
- Final todo state had all items completed.

Key constraints and preferences preserved from user:
- Do not expose junk/unavailable models in whitelist.
- Verify actual availability under current OpenAI/Codex auth, mindful that limits/quota may affect usability.
- Update the three named config files only where appropriate.
- Use OmO model-matching guidance to decide where `gpt-5.4-mini` is actually suitable.

Compressed block (b1) covered the full gpt-5.4-mini investigation, config edits, and runtime QA.

Completed work summary:
- `opencode/opencode.jsonc`: Added `gpt-5.4-mini` to `provider.openai.whitelist` and added full model definition with `context: 400000, output: 128000, input: [text, image], output: [text]` plus all reasoning variants (none/low/medium/high/xhigh).
- `opencode/oh-my-openagent_openai.jsonc`: Changed `prometheus` and `momus` from `gpt-5.4-mini` back to `gpt-5.4` (per OmO guide, these heavy roles shouldn't be on mini).
- `opencode/oh-my-openagent_openai-google-hybrid.jsonc`: No changes needed (already aligned).
- Verification: `opencode models openai` now lists `openai/gpt-5.4-mini`. Runtime log showed `providerID=openai modelID=gpt-5.4-mini stream` confirming model resolves under current OpenAI OAuth. Full completion not captured due to CLI timeout, but model is available and resolves.

User then asked for a simple CLI check command for the model, but that request was interrupted by the new task.

1. TASK: Search this dotfiles repo for every local reference to antigravity provider/models/plugins and summarize the effective configuration.
2. EXPECTED OUTCOME: Return concrete file paths with notes showing where antigravity plugin/auth is enabled or disabled, where antigravity model IDs are referenced, and any configs that must be updated together.
3. REQUIRED TOOLS: read, grep, glob only.
4. MUST DO: Focus on opencode configs, plugin lists, provider settings, and oh-my-openagent antigravity config files. Include evidence about whether the antigravity plugin is currently commented out or inactive.
5. MUST NOT DO: Do not edit files. Do not search unrelated directories. Do not speculate outside the repo.
6. CONTEXT: I need to determine why `opencode models google` does not list `google/antigravity-*` models even though Oh My OpenAgent antigravity configs reference them.

1. TASK: Inspect the two antigravity Oh My OpenAgent config files and identify any model IDs that look inconsistent, risky, or likely unavailable.

3. REQUIRED TOOLS: read, grep only.

5. MUST NOT DO: Do not edit files. Do not search external resources. Do not duplicate the first explore task's broader repo scan.

3. REQUIRED TOOLS: web docs, context7, official pages only.

5. MUST NOT DO: Do not edit files. Do not provide generic Google AI docs unless directly relevant to antigravity provider behavior.
6. CONTEXT: The local config currently does not show antigravity models in `opencode models google`, but the user has authorized multiple antigravity Google accounts and wants those models probed and whitelisted.

User request: probe Google Antigravity models under current auth, add them to whitelist if actually available, and re-check `oh-my-openagent_antigravity-opus_4_6.jsonc` plus `oh-my-openagent_antigravity-flash_only.jsonc`.

Known context:
- `opencode models google` currently lists only standard Gemini models: `gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-2.5-flash-lite`, `gemini-2.5-pro`, `gemini-3-flash-preview`, `gemini-3-pro-preview`, `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`.
- Grep for `antigravity` in `opencode models` returned nothing.
- In `opencode/opencode.jsonc`, the plugin list previously had `// "opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.0",` commented out.
- The Google provider whitelist currently contains only normal Gemini model IDs and has `only_defined_models: true` plus `discover: false`.
- Two antigravity OmO config files are already read and contain model IDs such as `google/antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking-high`, `google/antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking-medium`, `google/antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking-high`, `google/antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking-medium`, `google/antigravity-gemini-3-flash`, `google/antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro-high`, `google/antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro-low`.

Need from you:
- Produce a precise step-by-step plan.
- Define success criteria and manual QA steps.
- Identify the minimum safe edits depending on whether the antigravity plugin is currently disabled vs enabled but whitelisted out.
- Note what to do if model IDs in the OmO configs do not match provider-exposed IDs.

Do not edit files. Return concise actionable steps.

content.summary:
User asked to probe Google Antigravity models, add them to whitelist in `opencode.jsonc`, and verify two OmO antigravity configs.

State before user hint:
- `opencode models google` listed only standard Gemini models, zero antigravity models.
- `opencode models 2>&1 | grep -i "antigravity"` returned empty.
- In `opencode/opencode.jsonc` plugin list: `// "opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.0",` was COMMENTED OUT.
- Google provider had `only_defined_models: true` and `discover: false`.
- Google whitelist contained only: `gemini-3.1-pro-preview`, `gemini-3-pro-preview`, `gemini-3-flash-preview`, `gemini-2.5-pro`, `gemini-2.5-flash`, `gemini-2.5-flash-lite`.
- Google models section had definitions for those 6 models only.

Two OmO antigravity configs read:
1. `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode/oh-my-openagent_antigravity-opus_4_6.jsonc` uses models: `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking-high`, `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking-medium`, `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking-high`, `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking-medium`, `antigravity-gemini-3-flash`, `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro-high`, `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro-low`.
2. `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode/oh-my-openagent_antigravity-flash_only.jsonc` uses only `antigravity-gemini-3-flash` for everything.

Background tasks fired but not yet collected: `bg_33638bf0` (explore locals), `bg_70fdcc1a` (compare configs), `bg_dcabbf0a` (research external). Plan agent `ses_29bc4d697ffeX7jNF4YQYYiBR0` returned but output not yet retrieved.

Key issue identified: antigravity plugin was commented out in config, which explains why models were invisible.

Background tasks completed. User provided the CRITICAL HINT — the actual antigravity model definitions and plugin config:

Plugin must be enabled: `"opencode-antigravity-auth@latest"`

Actual antigravity model IDs and specs (from user's hint config):
- `antigravity-gemini-3-pro` — limit 1048576/65535, variants: low/high (thinkingLevel)
- `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro` — limit 1048576/65535, variants: low/high (thinkingLevel)
- `antigravity-gemini-3-flash` — limit 1048576/65536, variants: minimal/low/medium/high (thinkingLevel)
- `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6` — limit 200000/64000, no variants
- `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking` — limit 200000/64000, variants: low (thinkingBudget 8192) / max (thinkingBudget 32768)

Plus standard Gemini models also available through antigravity provider (but user wants them kept separate with `antigravity-` prefix to avoid confusion with normal Google auth models).

User explicitly said: "только они должны называться с применением слова antigravity, чтобы не спутались с теми которые из gemini авторизации" — meaning antigravity models MUST keep the `antigravity-` prefix in model IDs.

**IMPORTANT DISCREPANCY**: The OmO antigravity configs use model IDs like `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking-high` and `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking-medium`, but the actual provider exposes `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking` with variants `low`/`max`. Similarly, configs use `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro-high`/`antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro-low` but the provider has `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro` with variants. And `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking-high`/`antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking-medium` but provider has `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6` without thinking/variants. These naming mismatches need resolution.

Work plan: uncomment plugin, add antigravity models to whitelist and models section, fix OmO config model ID mismatches, runtime QA.

All background task IDs for cleanup: `bg_33638bf0`, `bg_70fdcc1a`, `bg_dcabbf0a`. Plan session: `ses_29bc4d697ffeX7jNF4YQYYiBR0`.

Fix oh-my-openagent_antigravity-opus_4_6.jsonc model IDs to match actual provider
Fix oh-my-openagent_antigravity-flash_only.jsonc model IDs to match actual provider
Runtime QA: opencode models google shows antigravity models + test invocation

content.summary:
New task: user asked in Russian to probe Google Antigravity models after authorizing several Antigravity Google accounts, add them to whitelist, and re-check `@oh-my-openagent_antigravity-opus_4_6.jsonc` plus `@oh-my-openagent_antigravity-flash_only.jsonc`. User later added a crucial hint config showing the exact plugin and model IDs, and explicitly said the Antigravity models must keep the `antigravity` naming so they are not confused with models coming from normal Gemini auth: "только они должны называться с применением слова antigravity, чтобы не спутались с теми которые из gemini авторизации".

Files already read in full:

Critical mismatch identified between the existing OmO Antigravity config files and the actual provider-exposed model IDs:

(b5)

After that earlier antigravity prep state, the assistant collected pending background outputs and the final plan output:
- `bg_70fdcc1a` (compare antigravity configs) returned no useful text.
- `bg_33638bf0` reaffirmed the need to understand why antigravity models were missing locally.
- `bg_dcabbf0a` returned the key external facts from the official `opencode-antigravity-auth` docs: plugin must be enabled, model discovery is config-defined rather than automatic, and the expected Google provider model IDs are exactly:
  - `antigravity-gemini-3-pro` with variants `low/high`
  - `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro` with variants `low/high`
  - `antigravity-gemini-3-flash` with variants `minimal/low/medium/high`
  - `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6` (no variants)
  - `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking` with variants `low/max`
  It also noted standard Gemini CLI models exist, but the user had explicitly requested that the antigravity-named models stay distinct from normal Gemini auth models.
- The continued plan agent session `ses_29bc4d697ffeX7jNF4YQYYiBR0` summarized the diagnosis and success criteria: plugin enabled, Google provider locked by whitelist + `only_defined_models: true`, antigravity models currently invisible; fix `opencode.jsonc`, then align the OmO antigravity preset files, then runtime QA.

User reminder/instruction that governed implementation: antigravity models must be named with `antigravity` to avoid confusion with normal Gemini-auth models.

Fresh read of `/Users/tim/dev/dotfiles/opencode/opencode.jsonc` lines 1-40 proved:
- `"opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.0"` was already enabled in the `plugin` array.
- `// "opencode-gemini-auth@latest",` was commented out.
This corrected the earlier plan wording that said “uncomment plugin”; the real work was only to expose model IDs through Google provider config.

Implementation decisions made explicit before editing:
- Treat the user-provided hint config plus librarian docs as source of truth for real provider IDs.
- Because the OmO Antigravity preset `oh-my-openagent_antigravity-opus_4_6.jsonc` used non-existent suffix-based IDs such as `...-thinking-high`, `...-thinking-medium`, `...-3.1-pro-high`, and `...-3.1-pro-low`, normalize it to the provider’s real base IDs plus `variant` fields where appropriate.
- Leave `oh-my-openagent_antigravity-flash_only.jsonc` unchanged unless QA shows otherwise, because it already uses the valid base ID `google/antigravity-gemini-3-flash`.
- For `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking`, map the old pseudo-`high` usages to `variant: "max"` and pseudo-`medium` usages to `variant: "low"`, since the provider only supports `low` and `max`.
- For `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6-thinking-*`, collapse to plain `google/antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6` because the provider does not expose thinking-suffixed Sonnet IDs.
- For `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro-high/low`, use base model `google/antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro` plus `variant: "high"` / `variant: "low"`.

Todos were updated to the shorter execution set:
1. Add antigravity models to Google whitelist/models in `opencode.jsonc` (initially phrased as uncomment plugin + add models, then effectively completed without changing plugin line).
2. Fix `oh-my-openagent_antigravity-opus_4_6.jsonc` model IDs.
3. Fix/verify `oh-my-openagent_antigravity-flash_only.jsonc`.
4. Runtime QA.

content.summary:
(b6) covered the initial antigravity probing and user hint.

New work completed:
- `opencode/opencode.jsonc`:
  - Added 5 Antigravity models to `provider.google.whitelist`: `antigravity-gemini-3-pro`, `antigravity-gemini-3.1-pro`, `antigravity-gemini-3-flash`, `antigravity-claude-sonnet-4-6`, `antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking`.
  - Added full model definitions in `provider.google.models` using the user-provided hint (limits, modalities, and variants like `thinkingLevel` or `thinkingBudget`).
- `opencode/oh-my-openagent_antigravity-opus_4_6.jsonc`:
  - Normalized all model IDs to the real provider IDs.
  - Replaced suffix-based IDs (e.g., `...-thinking-high`) with base model + `variant` (e.g., `google/antigravity-claude-opus-4-6-thinking` + `variant: "max"`).
  - Collapsed `modelConcurrency` to base model IDs.
- `opencode/oh-my-openagent_antigravity-flash_only.jsonc`: Verified, no changes needed as it already used the valid `google/antigravity-gemini-3-flash`.

Verification results:
- `opencode models google` now correctly lists the 5 Antigravity models.
- Smoke tests (`opencode run -m google/antigravity-gemini-3-flash`) revealed that the plugin `opencode-antigravity-auth@1.6.0` fails to load due to a runtime error: `Missing 'default' export in module ... proper-lockfile/index.js`.
- This explains the `AI_LoadAPIKeyError` — the auth provider is not active.

Next step: Update `opencode-antigravity-auth` to `@latest` in `opencode.jsonc` to resolve the loader error.
